source: Greenbarz natgas pulse · Aggregated hub-level price snapshots refreshed weekly during launch period, then quarterly. Sourced from independent commodity assessor benchmarks.
note: US natgas regional pulse. We track ~14 of the most-watched hubs across 4 thematic buckets — not every delivery point in every pipeline. The signal is regional rotation and basis stress, not exhaustive coverage.
scope: US Natural Gas · 14 hubs · 4 thematic buckets

benchmark.name: Henry Hub
benchmark.context: The US natgas benchmark. Henry is the NYMEX delivery point and the reference price for nearly every other hub. When Henry moves, it ripples through every basis differential.

mood.label: BASIS STRESS
mood.headline: Permian gas crashing into negative territory while Northeast premiums hold firm — classic spring shoulder season divergence.

hero_signals.headline: Waha at NEGATIVE $4.41
hero_signals.subhead: Permian producers PAYING to move gas
hero_signals.body: Waha is trading at a $7+ discount to Henry. With oil-associated gas overwhelming pipeline takeaway, producers are paying buyers to take volumes. This is the loudest US natgas signal right now.

hero_signals.headline: Algonquin Citygate +35% WoW
hero_signals.body: Algonquin trading $0.37 over Henry. Northeast pipeline constraints + late-season heating demand pushing citygate prices well above the national benchmark. Watch for a shoulder-season cooldown.

hero_signals.headline: Houston Ship Channel firm
hero_signals.body: HSC at $2.42 — slight discount to Henry but firmer than Permian. Gulf Coast LNG terminals continuing to pull volumes south. The spread to Waha tells you everything about where US gas wants to flow.

buckets.title: Gulf Benchmarks

gb_take.title: GB Read
gb_take.body: The Permian-Northeast spread is the loudest US natgas signal right now. With Waha at -$4 and Algonquin at +$3, you're looking at a $7+ regional dispersion — wider than most of 2024-25. This screams pipeline-constrained supply at the wellhead and unmet demand at the citygate. Watch two things: Matterhorn and Permian Highway flow rates (any expansion eases Waha) and Northeast shoulder-season cooldown (premiums collapse when heating demand fades). LNG demand at HSC remains the structural bid that prevents Gulf prices from chasing Permian into negative territory.
